name: AACR Gertrude B. Elion Cancer Research Award
awardAmount: $225,000 USD (total)
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle: LOI June 4, 2026 1:00 PM ET (passed); full application (by invitation) September 16, 2026 1:00 PM ET. Grant term begins Nov 1, 2026. Next LOI cycle expected ~June 2027.
eligibility: Early-career faculty holding a tenure-eligible (or equivalent) appointment at the rank of assistant professor who started their first independent faculty position within the past 6 years. Tenured faculty and those at associate professor rank are ineligible. Must be an AACR Active member in good standing by the LOI deadline.
description: AACR career-development award for a scientist in the first six years of an independent faculty position. Provides $225,000 over one year. No citizenship or geographic restrictions, though non-citizens must ensure their visa permits project completion. Requires AACR Active membership. Verified open for the 2026 cycle; LOI (June 4, 2026) has passed, full application due September 16, 2026.

name: Research Grant
awardAmount: Up to $65,000 USD per year (standard track)
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle is CLOSED (LOI was March 16, 2026; full application April 20, 2026). Check dtrf.org in early 2027 for the next cycle (LOI historically ~mid-March).
eligibility: Investigators proposing innovative and translational desmoid tumor research (basic science through clinical/correlative studies). Now offered under DTRF's consolidated 'Investigator-Initiated Research Proposals' framework, which also includes a Pilot Funding track (up to $30,000/yr for 1 year) for investigators new to desmoid research. Early-stage investigators are strongly encouraged. DTRF does not fund indirect costs.
description: DTRF's main investigator-initiated research grant (standard track, up to $65,000/yr for 1-3 years). 2026 cycle is closed; program recurs annually with an LOI typically due mid-March and full applications in April. No citizenship requirement stated (foundation funds internationally). 2026 dates: LOI Mar 16, full app Apr 20, awards announced Aug 21, projects start ~Nov 1, 2026. Next cycle opens early 2027.

name: Young Investigator Awards
awardAmount: $75,000 USD per year ($225,000 total); does not cover institutional indirect costs
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle is CLOSED (portal opened Jan 12, 2026; applications due Feb 23, 2026 3:00 PM ET). Next cycle expected ~Jan-Feb 2027.
eligibility: Early-career investigators (basic scientists, medical oncologists, pathologists, urologists, radiologists, radiation oncologists, public-health experts, bioinformaticians, bioengineers, and others) in academic research settings. At least one mentor is required. Recipients must attend the annual PCF Scientific Retreat and Young Investigator Forum.
description: PCF's flagship early-career mentored award: $75,000/yr for 3 years to support promising young prostate cancer investigators. Mentorship required. No citizenship restriction stated. 2026 cycle closed (deadline Feb 23, 2026); recurs annually with the portal typically opening in January and a late-February deadline. Next cycle ~early 2027.

name: Distinguished Scientist Awards
awardAmount: $750,000 USD total ($150,000 per year)
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle is CLOSED (portal opened Jan 12, 2026; application deadline March 18, 2026 11:59 PM ET; awards announced Fall 2026). Next cycle expected ~Jan-March 2027.
eligibility: Early-career faculty in brain cancer/brain tumor research at institutions in the United States or Canada. First independent faculty appointment no earlier than March 1, 2021 and no later than Jan 1, 2026 (roughly first 5 years). MD and/or PhD (or equivalent) with a brain-tumor research track record. Traditional clinical trials generally not supported.
description: Major career-development award for early-career brain cancer researchers: $750,000 over 5 years, emphasizing support of the person over a single project. Applicant must hold a faculty position at a US or Canadian institution, but US/Canadian citizenship is NOT required. 2026 cycle closed (deadline Mar 18, 2026); recurs annually with the portal opening ~January. Next cycle ~early 2027.

name: Collaborative Research Development Grant
funder: Ovarian Cancer Research Alliance (OCRA, formerly OCRFA)
awardAmount: Up to $300,000 USD per year ($900,000 total)
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle is CLOSED (LOI due March 25, 2026 5:00 PM ET; full proposal by invitation June 10, 2026; projects start Jan 1). Next cycle LOI expected ~March 2027.
eligibility: All investigators/collaborators must hold full-time faculty appointments at a school of medicine or public health or a recognized non-profit research facility. Designed for multi-investigator / multi-institution collaborations; interdisciplinary and international collaborations welcome. (Note: this is a collaborative/team grant for established faculty rather than a strictly early-career award.)
description: OCRA (formerly Ovarian Cancer Research Fund Alliance) grant funding collaborative ovarian/gynecologic cancer research across investigators within or between institutions. Up to $300,000/yr for 3 years. Requires full-time faculty appointments; international collaborations welcome (no citizenship restriction). 2026 cycle closed (LOI Mar 25, 2026); recurs annually with LOI typically in late March. Next cycle ~early 2027.

name: Prostate Cancer Research Program (PCRP) Early Investigator Research Award
funder: U.S. Department of Defense — Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs (CDMRP)
awardAmount: Up to $630,000 total costs
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle open: pre-application (mandatory) due Aug 12, 2026; full application due Sept 2, 2026. Recurs annually.
eligibility: Early-career investigators holding a doctoral degree (or equivalent) with fewer than 3 years of postdoctoral research experience at the submission deadline (excluding clinical residency/fellowship). Requires at least one mentor experienced in prostate cancer research. No citizenship requirement.
description: Mentored research award supporting investigators in the earliest stages of their independent careers; applications must address an FY26 PCRP Overarching Challenge and include a researcher development plan. Administered via eBRAP/Grants.gov. (Note: some Grants.gov listings now label the sponsor 'DoW / Department of War' following a DoD rebrand, but the program identity is unchanged.)

name: Prostate Cancer Research Program (PCRP) Physician Research Award
funder: U.S. Department of Defense — Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs (CDMRP)
awardAmount: Up to $1.1M total costs
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle open: pre-application (mandatory) due Aug 12, 2026; full application due Sept 2, 2026. Recurs annually.
eligibility: Early-career clinician-investigators: in the final year of an accredited medical residency/fellowship, OR within 5 years of initiating a first faculty appointment (including instructor). Requires at least one mentor experienced in prostate cancer research. No citizenship requirement.
description: Mentored research experience to prepare physicians with clinical duties for independent careers in prostate cancer research; applications must address an FY26 PCRP Overarching Challenge and include a research/career development plan. Administered via eBRAP/Grants.gov.

name: CRI Irvington Postdoctoral Fellowship Program
awardAmount: 3-year stipend of $74,000 / $76,000 / $78,000 (years 1-3) plus $5,000/year institutional allowance
deadlineInfo: Two cycles per year; next deadlines Sept 1, 2026 and March 1, 2027 (11:59 p.m. ET).
eligibility: Must hold a doctoral degree by award activation and have fewer than 5 years of relevant postdoctoral research experience at activation (MD residency years excluded). Research must be in immunology or tumor/cancer immunology under a qualified sponsor (assistant professor or higher) at a nonprofit U.S. or foreign institution. No citizenship restrictions — open to U.S. and non-U.S. citizens.
description: Flagship CRI postdoctoral fellowship funding hypothesis-driven research in immunology and cancer immunology. International applicants welcome; funding period is three years.

name: Postdoctoral Fellowship
awardAmount: $195,000 total over 3 years ($60,000/year PI salary + fringe, plus $5,000/year for supplies/professional development)
deadlineInfo: Grant year 2027 (CY2026) cycle: application due Sept 3, 2026, 5:00 p.m. EST. No LOI required. Recurs annually.
eligibility: Clinical and postdoctoral fellows; clinical fellows must have completed at least 24 months and no more than 72 months of fellowship. Minimum 80% research effort; lab- or clinic-based lymphoma projects. Must be based at a U.S. or Canadian institution and be a U.S./Canadian citizen or hold a valid work visa (citizenship not strictly required, but work authorization is).
description: Early-career fellowship supporting lymphoma research; special initiatives designate at least one award each for follicular lymphoma and mantle cell lymphoma projects, plus one for an applicant from an underrepresented group. Notification by ~Dec of application year.

name: Clinical Investigator Career Development Award
awardAmount: $225,000 total over 3 years ($70,000/year salary + $5,000/year incidentals)
deadlineInfo: Grant year 2027 (CY2026) cycle: application due Sept 3, 2026, 5:00 p.m. EST. No LOI required. Recurs annually.
eligibility: Clinical fellows and faculty only — licensed clinical physicians in ACGME-accredited specialties. Minimum 35-50% research time; project must involve clinical observation of human subjects. Must be based at a U.S. or Canadian institution and be a U.S./Canadian citizen or hold a valid work visa (citizenship not strictly required, but work authorization is).
description: Career development award providing salary support and protected research time for physician-investigators pursuing lymphoma research careers. A minimum of one CDA is designated for a project focused on underrepresented patient populations and/or an applicant from an underrepresented group.

name: Young Investigator Award
awardAmount: $50,000 (one year)
deadlineInfo: Annual general competition; application typically due late September (the 2026 grant-year deadline was ~Sept 23, 2025). Next (2027 grant-year) deadline expected ~late Sept 2026 — exact date not yet confirmed on funder site.
eligibility: Physician (MD, DO, or international equivalent) within the last two years of final oncology subspecialty training/fellowship at an academic medical institution and within 10 years of obtaining the medical degree at time of submission. Requires a mentor and ASCO membership (or pending application). Open to U.S. and international applicants in any country.
description: Mentored one-year grant funding physicians transitioning from fellowship to a faculty appointment to pursue clinical oncology research. Applications submitted via the ASCO/Conquer Cancer portal (asco.smapply.org). Next-cycle exact deadline not yet posted, so no concrete date verified.

name: AACR-Conquer Cancer Foundation of ASCO Young Investigator Award for Translational Cancer Research
awardAmount: $50,000 (one year)
deadlineInfo: Annual; application typically due mid-December (the 2026 grant-year deadline was Dec 18, 2025, now closed). Next (2027 grant-year) deadline expected ~mid-Dec 2026 — exact date not yet confirmed on funder site.
eligibility: MD, DO, MD/PhD, or equivalent medical degree; in the final two years of subspecialty training and within 10 years of obtaining the medical degree at the start of the grant term. At least 60% full-time research effort; must be an AACR member in good standing. No citizenship or geographic requirements (non-citizens must hold a visa permitting completion of the project).
description: Co-sponsored AACR / Conquer Cancer award funding a physician-scientist during the transition from a fellowship program to a faculty appointment, promoting translational oncology research. Program remains active; next-cycle exact deadline not yet posted, so no concrete date verified.

name: Fellowship
funder: Jane Coffin Childs Memorial Fund for Medical Research
awardAmount: Stipend $72,000 (yr1) / $74,000 (yr2) / $76,000 (yr3); plus $2,000/yr lab research allowance, $1,800 one-time travel allowance, and $1,000/yr per dependent child under 18
deadlineInfo: Annual — application portal opens Oct 1, 2026; application deadline Feb 1, 2027 at 3:00 PM EST (decisions mid-May 2027, fellowships start July 1, 2027)
eligibility: Postdoctoral researchers; PhD conferred no more than 18 months (MD no more than 3 years) before the Feb 1 deadline and no more than 1 year of prior postdoctoral experience. Citizens of any country eligible; awards to non-U.S. citizens can only be made to sponsoring institutions in the United States (U.S. citizens may hold the award in the U.S. or abroad).
description: Prestigious independent postdoctoral fellowship in the biomedical sciences (broad remit, not cancer-specific). Requires three referee letters (advisor/co-advisor cannot be a referee), a proposed advisor form, and institutional signature. Next cycle: portal opens Oct 1, 2026 with a Feb 1, 2027 deadline.

name: Young Investigator Award
awardAmount: $100,000 total over 2 years (indirect costs not allowable)
deadlineInfo: Annual — 2026 cycle closed; next cycle opens ~January 2027 with LOI ~February 2027 and full application ~March 2027
eligibility: Early-career researchers within 6 years of completing a professional degree or clinical training (e.g., senior postdoctoral fellow, instructor, research associate, or assistant-professor equivalent); may not hold the title of Associate Professor or higher. Must hold a medical and/or doctoral degree and have an approved mentor. Applicant must be at a U.S. or Canadian academic, non-profit, or governmental institution.
description: Two-year, $100,000 award for early-career bladder cancer researchers (basic, translational, clinical, epidemiologic, bioengineering). No explicit citizenship requirement, but the sponsoring institution must be in the U.S. or Canada. Cycle runs LOI (Feb) then full application (Mar); 2026 cycle is closed.

name: PCF Young Investigators
awardAmount: $225,000 total ($75,000 per year for 3 years)
deadlineInfo: Annual — 2026 cycle closed (portal opened Jan 12, 2026; deadline Feb 23, 2026, 3:00 PM EDT); 2027 RFA not yet posted, next cycle expected ~Jan–Feb 2027
eligibility: Early-career investigators, generally age 35 and younger, in postdoctoral fellowships or recently appointed to junior faculty positions; must work under the direct supervision of 1–3 mentors; should not previously have been PI on a major national grant (e.g., R01) though smaller/career-development awards are acceptable. Open to the global biomedical research community across disciplines.
description: Three-year mentored award for early-career prostate cancer researchers worldwide. The 2026 RFA closed Feb 23, 2026; PCF runs this annually and a 2027 RFA is expected but not yet published, so no concrete next date can be verified.

name: International Research Fellowship Program
awardAmount: $60,000 stipend to cover living expenses (per recent guidelines; confirm current amount on funder site)
deadlineInfo: Annual — application deadline historically ~February 28; current cycle closed, next call expected to open ~January 2027 (verify on funder site)
eligibility: Postdoctoral scientists who received an advanced degree (PhD, DSc, or DVM) no more than three years before the application deadline. Program is for young Italian scientists conducting cancer research (basic, translational, or clinical) at institutions in the United States.
description: One-year postdoctoral fellowship supporting young Italian scientists doing cancer research in U.S. labs (AICF has funded 580+ awards since 1983). Note: U.S. citizenship is NOT required, but the program targets Italian citizens/scientists working in the U.S. The original JHU-listed URL (research-fellowships.html) currently returns HTTP 404; details drawn from AICF site and recent call postings, so verify the current cycle and stipend directly with AICF (fellowship@americanitaliancancer.org).

name: Immuno-Informatics Postdoctoral Fellowship
awardAmount: $243,000 total over 3 years
deadlineInfo: Biannual — next deadline September 1, 2026 (following deadline March 1, 2027)
eligibility: Outstanding early-career (postdoctoral) scientists seeking dual expertise in immunology and data science. No citizenship restrictions (same core eligibility framework as the CRI Irvington fellowship).
description: CRI fellowship for postdocs building combined expertise in immunology and data science; shares the CRI postdoctoral fellowship application cycles (Sep 1 and Mar 1) and $243,000 total support over 3 years. Open to all nationalities.

name: Damon Runyon Physician-Scientist Training Award
awardAmount: $460,000 total over four years ($100K/$110K/$120K/$130K by year), plus up to $100,000 medical school debt repayment and $1,000/child annually
deadlineInfo: December 1, 2026, 4:00 pm ET (annual application deadline)
eligibility: Open only to MD or DO degree holders (MD/PhDs NOT eligible; cannot pursue a PhD). Must have completed clinical training and be U.S. Specialty Board eligible before award start, with no more than 3 years of postdoctoral research experience, and must apply before an initial assistant professorship. Requires >=80% research effort and a qualified mentor. Early-career physician-scientist (postdoc) stage.
description: Four-year award recruiting U.S. Specialty Board-eligible physicians (MD/DO only) into cancer research careers. Verified open on the official Damon Runyon page with a concrete next deadline of December 1, 2026. No explicit citizenship requirement (research conducted at a U.S. institution; U.S. Specialty Board eligibility required).

name: Young Investigator Award (YIA)
awardAmount: Up to $200,000 for up to 2 years (inclusive of 10% indirect costs)
deadlineInfo: annual; most recent (2026) cycle LOI due Dec 2, 2025 and full application Jan 5 - Feb 2, 2026 (award start July 1, 2026). Next cycle LOI expected ~early December 2026 - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Early-career neurofibromatosis (NF) researchers - senior/advanced doctoral students and postdoctoral fellows - seeking to establish themselves as independent NF investigators. No citizenship requirement (international applicants eligible).
description: CTF award funding senior doctoral students and postdocs to become independent NF (neurofibromatosis) investigators. As of July 2026 the 2026 cycle (LOI Dec 2, 2025; full app Jan-Feb 2026) has closed; program recurs annually with LOI typically in early December, but the next concrete date is not yet posted. No citizenship restriction.

name: Basic Research Fellowship Program
awardAmount: $100,000 over two years (mentored fellowship)
deadlineInfo: annual; most recent (2026) cycle LOI due December 10, 2025, with full applications by invitation in early 2026. Next cycle LOI expected ~December 2026 - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Postdoctoral fellows conducting laboratory or field-based brain tumor research. Requires a lead mentor holding a full-time faculty appointment at the same institution who currently conducts brain tumor research (one application per lead mentor per cycle). Applicants are not required to be U.S. citizens or residents.
description: Two-year, $100,000 mentored postdoctoral fellowship for brain-tumor research. As of July 2026 the 2026 cycle (LOI due Dec 10, 2025) has closed; recurs annually with LOI typically in early-mid December, but the next concrete date is not yet posted. Open to non-citizens/non-residents.

name: Young Investigator Grant
awardAmount: Up to $60,000 per year for 3 years (up to $180,000 total)
deadlineInfo: annual; most recent (2026) cycle proposals due December 11, 2025 (8:00 pm ET), notifications ~May 2026. Next cycle expected ~December 2026 - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Applicants may apply during fellowship training or early in their research career but must not hold an appointment higher than Instructor. Time limits: PhD applicants within 4 years of terminal degree (or within first 3 years at Instructor if in an accredited clinical fellowship); MD/MD-PhD applicants within 7 years of last doctoral degree. PI must be employed by a non-profit institution. No citizenship requirement.
description: ALSF grant supporting early-career (fellow/Instructor-level) investigators in childhood cancer research; up to $180,000 over 3 years. As of July 2026 the 2026 cycle (proposals due Dec 11, 2025) has closed; recurs annually ~December. No citizenship requirement, but PI must be at a non-profit institution. Note: the previously listed URL (early-career-research-programs) no longer resolves; current grant page is grant-categories/young-investigator.

name: AACR Immuno-Oncology Research Fellowships
awardAmount: $130,000 over two years
deadlineInfo: annual; most recent verifiable (2025) cycle deadline was July 29, 2025, 1:00 pm ET via ProposalCentral. 2026 cycle deadline not yet posted; historically ~late July - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Postdoctoral or clinical research fellows who completed their doctoral degree within the past 5 years, conducting a mentored immuno-oncology project (basic, translational, clinical, or population science). Applicant must be an AACR member in good standing (or submit a satisfactory membership application by the deadline). Stated: 'There are no citizenship or geographic requirements' (non-U.S. applicants must have sufficient visa status).
description: AACR fellowship ($130,000 over 2 years) for postdoc/clinical fellows in immuno-oncology. NOT discontinued: the originally listed URL now 404s, but the program remains active at a new URL (aacr-immuno-oncology-research-fellowship-2). Recurs annually; last confirmed deadline July 29, 2025, so the 2026 cycle date (typically late July) should be verified on the AACR site. No citizenship requirement.

name: Intramural Continuing Umbrella of Research Experiences Program
awardAmount: Paid fellowship - NIH Intramural Research Program monthly stipend set by education level and relevant experience (per NIH IRP stipend tables)
duration: Postdoctoral fellows up to 3 years; postbaccalaureate/post-master's and graduate students up to 2 years
deadlineInfo: annual; most recent (FY2026) application period was open through February 16, 2026 (referee letters due Feb 23, 2026), for start dates by September 1, 2026. Next (FY2027) cycle expected ~February 2027 - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Postdoctoral fellows with no more than 5 years of relevant post-doctorate research experience (also open to recent college graduates, post-master's individuals, and graduate students who have achieved candidacy). Postdoc track requires a PhD/MD/DDS/DMD/DVM or equivalent by program start. Must be a U.S. citizen, non-citizen national, or U.S. permanent resident.
description: NCI intramural paid-fellowship (iCURE) program supporting trainees from groups underrepresented in biomedical research within NCI's intramural program. As of July 2026 the FY2026 window (through Feb 16, 2026) has closed; the program remains active and recurs annually (~February). Requires U.S. citizenship, non-citizen national status, or permanent residency. Canonical current page: trainatnci.cancer.gov/programs/icure.

name: AHNS Pilot Grant
awardAmount: $20,000 for one year (one award per year)
deadlineInfo: annual (AAO-HNS CORE cycle); most recent (2026) cycle LOI due December 15, 2025 and full application due January 15, 2026 (notification ~June). Next cycle LOI expected ~December 2026 - verify on funder site.
eligibility: Candidates must reside in the U.S. or Canada and be medical students, residents, or MD faculty at the rank of associate professor or below, and be an AHNS member in good standing. Prior AHNS/AAO-HNS Foundation grantees may reapply, but candidates already funded by a private or federal agency for the same research are ineligible. No U.S. citizenship requirement (U.S./Canada residency required).
description: AHNS Pilot Grant ($20,000/1 year) for basic, translational, or clinical head-and-neck oncology research, administered through the AAO-HNS CORE program on ProposalCentral. As of July 2026 the 2026 cycle (LOI Dec 15, 2025; full app Jan 15, 2026) has closed; recurs annually. Requires U.S./Canada residency and AHNS membership (no citizenship requirement). Note: intended for medical students, residents, and junior MD faculty rather than pure postdocs.

name: Career Development Program - Special Fellow
funder: Blood Cancer United (formerly The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society)
awardAmount: Up to $75,000 per year (minimum $70,000/year stipend/salary to awardee); additional childcare stipend up to $3,600/year
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2025-26 cycle: eligibility Oct 17, 2025; LOI Nov 1, 2025; full application Nov 15, 2025. Next cycle expected ~Oct-Nov 2026 (2026-27 dates not yet posted).
eligibility: PhD, MD, DVM, or equivalent; postdocs/instructors with 3-5 years of postdoctoral research training by award start; in a mentored research position doing hematologic-malignancy-relevant research. No citizenship requirement.
description: Formerly the LLS Career Development Program; the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society rebranded as Blood Cancer United. The Special Fellow track supports later-stage postdocs/instructors (3-5 yrs postdoc) needing additional mentored training to become competitive for independence. Open to applicants worldwide, including non-US citizens and those conducting research outside the US.

name: Scholar-in-Training Awards: Annual Meeting
awardAmount: Travel/attendance support for the AACR Annual Meeting (monetary award plus certificate; amount varies by sponsor)
duration: One-time award for the Annual Meeting
deadlineInfo: Annual, tied to AACR Annual Meeting abstract submission (~mid-November before the spring meeting). For the 2026 Annual Meeting the abstract/application deadline was November 18, 2025; the 2027 deadline is expected ~November 2026.
eligibility: AACR Associate/Active member in good standing; graduate students, medical/clinical/postdoctoral fellows, or early-career investigators who submit a meritorious first-author abstract for the Annual Meeting. Applicant must first submit an abstract, then complete a separate award application.
description: Long-running (since 1986) travel-grant program recognizing outstanding young investigators presenting proffered papers at the AACR Annual Meeting; 179 awards were given for the 2026 meeting. This is the domestic/general Annual Meeting track (distinct from the Global Scholar-in-Training Awards, which require residing in an eligible country, and the AACR-CIHR track, which requires Canadian citizenship/PR). No citizenship requirement for the general Annual Meeting award.

name: NCI Pathway to Independence Award for Outstanding Early Stage Postdoctoral Researchers (K99/R00 - Independent Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
funder: National Institutes of Health (NIH) / National Cancer Institute (NCI)
awardAmount: K99 phase: up to $100,000/year salary + up to $30,000/year research development costs. R00 phase: up to $249,000/year total costs.
duration: Up to 5 years total (K99 mentored phase up to 2 years; R00 independent phase up to 3 years)
deadlineInfo: Standard due dates: Feb 13, 2026; Jun 15, 2026; Oct 14, 2026; Feb 17, 2027; Jun 15, 2027; Oct 14, 2027. Next upcoming deadline (as of July 2026): October 14, 2026.
eligibility: Research or clinical doctorate (PhD, MD, DO, etc.) in a mentored postdoctoral position; limited years of postdoctoral research experience at time of application (see NOFO). No citizenship requirement.
description: The original RFA-CA-22-035 has expired. NCI no longer participates in the NIH Parent K99/R00; this opportunity was reissued as PAR-25-135 (NCI Pathway to Independence Award, K99/R00 - Clinical Trial Not Allowed), which expires October 15, 2027. No independent clinical trials allowed, though gaining experience in a mentor-led trial is permitted. Open to non-US citizens with appropriate visa status.

name: Postdoctoral Fellowships
awardAmount: Stipends of $66,000, $68,000, and $70,000 in years 1-3, plus $4,000/year allowance for health insurance and conference costs; additional $1,500 final-year bonus for meeting attendance
deadlineInfo: Two cycles annually: June 1 and December 1 (next business day if on a weekend/holiday). Next upcoming deadline (as of July 2026): December 1, 2026.
eligibility: Terminal doctoral degree held 3 years or less (excluding clinical training) and 3 or fewer years of postdoctoral research training at time of application; must hold a postdoctoral (non-faculty) position at a US academic or eligible nonprofit institution. US citizenship NOT required - non-US citizens with appropriate visa/lawful immigration status are eligible.
description: Active and accepting applications. Supports early-stage postdoctoral researchers in any area of cancer research. Includes a parental-leave option (4-12 weeks paid, with extended term and continued stipend). Non-US citizens eligible if they hold appropriate visa/lawful immigration status at time of application; work must be at a US institution.

name: Damon Runyon-Rachleff Innovation Award
awardAmount: $400,000 (Stage 1: $200,000/yr for 2 yrs), with possible extension to $800,000 total over up to 4 years for top performers
duration: 2 years (Stage 1), extendable to up to 4 years
deadlineInfo: Annual (Stage 1). FY2026 application deadline was July 1, 2026 (now closed). Applications open in ProposalCentral ~May and are due ~July 1 each year; next cycle expected ~July 1, 2027.
eligibility: Early-career investigators with high-risk/high-reward ideas but insufficient preliminary data for traditional funding. Categories: tenure-track Assistant Professors within first 5 years; Clinical Instructors/Senior Clinical Fellows (MD/DO/MD-PhD); or Distinguished Fellows with dedicated lab space. Must conduct independent research at a U.S. institution.
description: Supports exceptionally creative early-career researchers pursuing high-risk/high-reward ideas with potential to transform cancer prevention, diagnosis, or treatment. Program is active and recurring. Note the full name is the Damon Runyon-Rachleff Innovation Award; funding begins Jan 1 following selection.

name: Pancreatic Cancer Research Program (PCARP) - Idea Development Award with Early Career Investigator Option
awardAmount: Up to $700,000 total costs (standard); up to $950,000 total costs with the Early-Career Investigator / Partnering PI option
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle: mandatory pre-application deadline was July 7, 2026 (now closed); full application deadline October 7, 2026, 11:59 p.m. ET. Recurring annually; next full cycle expected mid-2027.
eligibility: Independent investigators at any career stage. The Partnering PI option pairs an experienced Initiating PI (mentor) with an Early-Career Investigator (Partnering PI) to promote their career development in pancreatic cancer research. Requires preliminary data; does not fund clinical trials.
description: Supports innovative, high-risk/high-reward research to accelerate improvements in pancreatic cancer outcomes. The FY26 mechanism is the Idea Development Award, whose Partnering PI Option provides the Early-Career Investigator pairing referenced in the original listing. Active/recurring; note URL migrated from cdmrp.army.mil to cdmrp.health.mil.

name: Ovarian Cancer Research Program (OCRP) - Pilot Award
awardAmount: Up to $350,000 total costs
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle: pre-application (preproposal) deadline July 13, 2026, 5:00 p.m. ET; full application (by invitation) deadline October 1, 2026, 11:59 p.m. ET. Recurring annually.
eligibility: Supports exploration of innovative, untested concepts in ovarian cancer; preliminary data not required. Open to independent investigators at all career levels; postdoctoral and clinical fellows may also apply. Requires a mandatory preproposal; full application is by invitation only. Reviewers are blinded to investigator/organization names.
description: Pilot Award funding innovative high-risk/high-reward exploratory ovarian cancer research concepts. Active FY26 mechanism. Note URL migrated from cdmrp.health.mil/funding/ocrp.aspx to cdmrp.health.mil/funding/ocrp.

name: Medical Research Award
funder: Gabrielle's Angel Foundation for Cancer Research
awardAmount: $225,000 total (over 3 years)
deadlineInfo: Annual, invitation-only. Invitations are sent each spring to NCI-designated cancer centers only; submissions are due in August, with funding decisions made in Q1 of the following year. No open public deadline.
eligibility: Invitation-only process limited to NCI-designated cancer centers. Supports early-career researchers with independent research projects and preliminary results/publications from their own lab. (Prospective applicants should confirm institutional eligibility through their NCI-designated center.)
description: Annual Medical Research Awards fund cutting-edge blood cancer research (leukemia, lymphoma, and related hematologic malignancies) most likely to translate into clinical trials within 3-5 years; also supports integrative/botanical approaches. IMPORTANT: the originally listed URL (public.onc.jhmi.edu pilot-projects page) is a generic internal portal, not the funder; the official source is gabriellesangels.org. Program is active but invitation-only, not open application.

name: Cancer Research Grants Program
awardAmount: No fixed limit; amount requested must be reasonable and justified by project scope (indirect/overhead capped at 5%; equipment items capped at $5,000)
deadlineInfo: Three cycles per year with closing dates of April 30, August 31, and December 1. Next deadline as of mid-2026: August 31, 2026.
eligibility: Open to investigators at U.S.-based non-profit institutions. Supports early-stage projects by new cancer researchers as well as innovative approaches by established investigators aimed at new cancer treatments or cures. Eligibility is tied to a U.S. non-profit institution rather than personal citizenship.
description: Funds research aimed at discovering new treatments or cures for cancer at U.S. non-profit institutions, targeting new investigators or novel approaches by established researchers. Active, rolling three-cycle annual program; applications submitted online.

name: Pilot Study Program
funder: The Rivkin Center for Ovarian Cancer Research (now OCRA)
awardAmount: $100,000 over 2 years ($50,000/yr) per OCRA (historically ~$75,000 total under Rivkin)
deadlineInfo: Annual; continued as the OCRA 'Rivkin Pilot Grant'. Most recent (2027 cycle) LOI was ~March 25, 2026 via SmartSimple; next cycle expected ~March 2027.
eligibility: Open to investigators across all career stages pursuing innovative, early-stage ovarian cancer research (etiology, prevention, early detection, therapeutics, cancer biology). No citizenship requirement.
description: The Rivkin Pilot Study Program continues under OCRA as the 'Rivkin Pilot Grant' after the April 2025 Rivkin-OCRA combination. Provides seed funding to generate foundational data for larger studies. Submissions via OCRA's SmartSimple portal; LOI stage precedes full application.

name: St. Baldrick's Scholars
awardAmount: Up to $110,000/year
duration: 3 years minimum, with option for 2 additional years based on progress
deadlineInfo: Annual (Spring cycle). 2026 award: LOI was Dec 8, 2025, full application ~Feb 2026, funding begins July 1, 2026. Next cycle LOI expected ~early December 2026.
eligibility: Must hold (or will hold by award start) a full-time faculty position, for no longer than 7 years at award start. Project must have direct relevance to pediatric cancer (basic, clinical, translational, or epidemiological). No citizenship requirement stated.
description: The St. Baldrick's Scholar (Career Development) Award supports early-career faculty establishing independent pediatric-cancer research programs. Applications via ProposalCentral. Note: many institutions require internal limited-submission review before the LOI.

name: St. Baldrick's International Scholars
awardAmount: Up to ~$110,000-$120,000/year (guidelines vary by year)
duration: 3 years, with option for 2 additional years based on progress
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle per guidelines: LOI ~March 15, 2026, full application ~May 15, 2026. Next cycle expected ~2027.
eligibility: Early-career investigators conducting pediatric-cancer research outside the U.S., historically aimed at researchers from/returning to low- and middle-income countries (World Bank classification). Research may be laboratory, clinical, or epidemiological. Not restricted to U.S. citizens.
description: The International Scholar Award trains/supports pediatric-cancer researchers based outside the United States (historically low- and middle-income countries) to build research capacity in their home regions. Sources show some year-to-year variation in exact award amount ($110K vs $120K/yr) and dates; confirm against the current-year guidelines PDF.

name: PCF-Pfizer Challenge Awards
awardAmount: Up to $1,000,000 per team over 2 years (no indirect costs)
deadlineInfo: PCF Challenge Awards are recurring/annual; 2026 application deadline was March 16, 2026 (3:00 p.m. EDT); next cycle expected ~early 2027. The Pfizer co-branded award is not a current standalone RFA.
eligibility: Team science: a research team of at least 3 investigators from non-profit academic/community institutions, including at least one young investigator. Global; no citizenship requirement.
description: The recurring program is the PCF Challenge Awards (team-science grants for high-risk, transformative prostate cancer research; teams must include >=1 young investigator). The specifically Pfizer co-branded 'PCF-Pfizer Global Health Equity Challenge Award' was a one-time 2021-2022 collaboration (winners announced April 2022) and is not currently an open standalone RFA. As of the 2026 open RFAs page, PCF lists the 2026 PCF Challenge Awards, 2026 Young Investigator Awards ($75K/yr x3, deadline Feb 23, 2026), and 2026 TACTICAL Awards.

name: Career Development Program
funder: Sarcoma Alliance for Research through Collaboration (SARC)
awardAmount: $100,000 (second year contingent on progress and fund availability)
deadlineInfo: Paused — SARC states it is 'currently seeking funding to continue the SARC Career Development Award.' No open application cycle for 2026-2027.
eligibility: Within 5 years of first faculty appointment (Instructor or Assistant Professor), OR in the final year of clinical/postdoctoral fellowship. Research must be performed at a U.S. or Canadian institution; applicants from other countries welcome if research is done in the U.S./Canada. No citizenship requirement.
description: SARC's Career Development Award (Career Development Program) supports early-stage physician-scientists/scientists in sarcoma research. The program is currently paused: SARC's site indicates it is seeking philanthropic funding to resume the award, and no application deadline is posted for 2026-2027.

name: "A" Award
awardAmount: Up to $200,000 per year (up to $800,000 total per ALSF early-career page; some third-party listings cite lower figures, so verify on official guidelines)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2026 cycle: LOI ~Dec 2025, full application ~April 2026 (now passed). Next cycle expected 2027: LOI ~December 2026, full application ~April 2027, via proposalCENTRAL. No 2027 date officially posted yet.
eligibility: Early-career independent faculty (MD, MD/PhD, or PhD) within 10 years of terminal degree and within 5 years of first faculty appointment as Assistant Professor at time of application; must hold a position at a non-profit academic, medical, or research institution in the U.S. or Canada.
description: The 'A' Award supports early independent-career scientists establishing a pediatric oncology research program with an original project and strong institutional career-development support. Still active as a recurring early-career mechanism (part of ALSF's Early Career Research Programs). Applications via proposalCENTRAL. Note: I could not access the official 2026 guidelines PDF (server blocked automated fetch), and third-party summaries disagree on the exact award amount, so the funding figure should be confirmed on the ALSF site.

name: Research Grant Funding
awardAmount: Two levels: RE03 Emerging Investigator Research Grant $50,000; RE33 Research Grant $100,000
duration: See site (typically up to ~2 years; not stated on current page)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2026 cycle: applications opened Feb 15, 2026; LOI due May 18, 2026; full proposals due July 6, 2026 (both now passed); funding notification Nov 2026. Next cycle ~2027 (LOI ~mid-May). No 2027 date officially posted yet.
eligibility: Doctorally-prepared PI who is a registered oncology nurse actively involved in cancer patient care, education, or research. RE03 ($50,000) targets emerging-to-mid-career researchers who have not previously completed a study funded at $50,000+ as PI (fits early-career faculty); RE33 ($100,000) is for established researchers with $50,000+ prior funding history.
description: Funder has rebranded to the 'Oncology Nursing Foundation' (site now at onfgivesback.org; the old onsfoundation.org URL is stale but the program continues). The Research Grant remains an active recurring award; the early-career-appropriate tier is the RE03 Emerging Investigator Research Grant ($50,000). No explicit citizenship/residency restriction is stated on the page.

name: Research Grants
awardAmount: $50,000 per year (total $100,000)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle, now branded 'Impact Grants'. 2027 Impact Grants cycle is CLOSED (application deadline was May 20, 2026; decisions by Dec 4, 2026). Next cycle ~2028, deadline expected ~May 2027. No 2028 date officially posted yet.
eligibility: Promising scientists including early-career investigators, postdoctoral fellows, and assistant professors. Applicant institution/organization must be based in the United States. Investigators whose named mentors are tobacco-industry-funded are ineligible.
description: The Prevent Cancer Foundation Research Grants & Fellowships program is now presented as '2027 Impact Grants' (renamed/branded 'Impact Grants'; same funder and research-grant/fellowship mechanism). Two-year research grants at $50,000/year. Requires a U.S.-based applicant institution; no individual citizenship requirement stated (fellowship track is available to non-U.S.-citizens at U.S. institutions per third-party guidance). The 2027 cycle has already closed.

name: Proposals in Leukemia Research
funder: When Everyone Survives Leukemia Research Foundation
duration: 1 year (renewable for one or more additional years based on productivity)
deadlineInfo: Annual Request for Proposals; applications due ~June 15. 2026 RFP deadline was June 15, 2026 (now passed; decisions Sept 1, 2026, funding begins Oct 1, 2026). Next cycle expected ~June 15, 2027. No 2027 date officially posted yet.
eligibility: New and established investigators requesting support for laboratory, translational, or clinical research related to acute leukemia. Open to all career stages.
description: Active recurring RFP soliciting innovative acute-leukemia research. One-year $50,000 awards, renewable. Open to both new and established investigators (not restricted to early career). No citizenship/residency requirement specified in the RFP.

name: Rare Cancers Research Program Idea Development Award
awardAmount: Up to $490,000 total costs
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle open: required pre-application (preproposal) due 5:00 p.m. ET, August 19, 2026; full application due 11:59 p.m. ET, November 18, 2026 (via eBRAP/Grants.gov).
eligibility: Independent investigators at ALL career levels (not restricted to early career, unlike some CDMRP career-development mechanisms). Foreign and domestic organizations are eligible to apply. FY26 applications must address biology, preclinical research models, or therapy focus areas for rare cancers.
description: FY26 (fiscal year 2026) Rare Cancers Research Program is active, funded at ~$17.5M program-wide. The Idea Development Award supports new ideas in rare-cancer research; it is open to independent investigators at all career levels and to foreign and domestic organizations (no citizenship requirement). Pre-application deadline Aug 19, 2026 is the next actionable step; full application Nov 18, 2026.

name: CLRA Research Grants
awardAmount: $30,000 total (paid $15,000 in January, $10,000 in July pending mid-term report, $5,000 on completion)
deadlineInfo: Annual; applications due June 30. 2026 deadline was June 30, 2026 (now passed); final review at early-December board meeting; projects start January 1. Next deadline expected June 30, 2027. No 2027 date separately posted yet.
eligibility: No explicit career-stage restriction stated on the page (historically supports newer investigators studying leukemia causes/cures). Research must be based within the U.S.
description: Active recurring $30,000 one-year grant for leukemia research (causes of and cures for leukemia). Applications submitted through an online portal by June 30 annually. Research must be U.S.-based; no explicit citizenship requirement stated.

name: Lymphoma Scientific Research Mentoring Program (LSRMP)
awardAmount: Scholar grant $5,000/year x 2 years ($10,000 total, for professional development and project expenses; no salary/equipment); program-related travel and lodging also covered by the Foundation.
deadlineInfo: 2027 cycle: full applications due September 3, 2026, 5:00 pm ET (via ProposalCentral). Annual cycle.
eligibility: Early-career lymphoma researchers via two tracks. Clinical Track: clinical fellows (>=2nd year hem/onc fellowship) or junior faculty (Instructor/Assistant Professor <=5 yrs in rank). Lab/Translational Track: postdoctoral fellows (<=8 yrs post-PhD), clinical fellows with translational focus, or early faculty. All requirements must be met by March 1, 2027.
description: Track-based mentoring and educational program (intensive workshop + ongoing mentorship + follow-up) plus small Scholar research grants to help early-career clinical and lab/translational lymphoma researchers launch independent projects. Citizenship note: applicants must be a U.S. or Canadian citizen OR hold a visa to work in the U.S./Canada, and must remain at a U.S./Canadian institution throughout the award (no strict citizenship requirement).

name: Young Investigator Award (YIA)
funder: American Society for Clinical Oncology (ASCO) / Conquer Cancer, the ASCO Foundation
awardAmount: $50,000 (one-year grant supporting personnel/research expenses plus travel to the Conquer Cancer Grants & Awards Ceremony)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. Most recent verified deadline was Jan 29, 2026 (11:59 pm ET) for the topic-specific Special Competitions (Drug Development, Survivorship, Supportive Care, Geriatric Oncology). Next general/Special Competition cycle expected to open in 2026 with a deadline in early 2027 - see site.
eligibility: Mentored award for physicians transitioning from fellowship to a faculty appointment. Must be within 10 years of obtaining their medical degree, have completed (or be within 3 months of completing) subspecialty training, hold a valid active medical license in the country where research is conducted, and have a scientific mentor. Exception to the 10-year cutoff possible for foreign medical graduates.
description: Conquer Cancer YIA funds promising early-career physician-investigators in clinical oncology. Applications accepted from both U.S. and international applicants (no citizenship requirement). Submitted via the ASCO/Conquer Cancer application portal (asco.smapply.org). Note the related AACR-Conquer Cancer YIA for Translational Cancer Research runs on a separate deadline (Dec 18, 2025 for the 2026 term).

name: Ovarian Cancer Research Program - Ovarian Cancer Academy Early-Career Investigator Award
funder: Department of Defense (DoD) - Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs (CDMRP)
awardAmount: Up to $1.125 million total costs per award (CDMRP expects to fund approx. 4 awards)
deadlineInfo: FY26 (2026): Pre-application/LOI due September 15, 2026, 5:00 pm ET; full application due October 1, 2026, 11:59 pm ET (via eBRAP/Grants.gov).
eligibility: Early-career investigators within a defined window of training completion; requires an eligible Designated Mentor and preliminary data. Provides a research project plus a structured career-development program within the Ovarian Cancer Academy to establish independence.
description: DoD OCRP mechanism combining a research grant with a mentored career-development academy for early-career ovarian cancer investigators. Both foreign and domestic organizations are eligible; no U.S. citizenship requirement. One of five FY26 OCRP mechanisms (Pilot, Investigator-Initiated Research, Clinical Trial, and Clinical Trial Academy ECI awards share the same deadlines).

name: Kidney Cancer Research Program - Academy of Kidney Cancer Investigators Early-Career Scholar Award (AKCIECSA)
funder: Department of Defense (DoD) - Congressionally Directed Medical Research Programs (CDMRP)
awardAmount: Up to $1.2 million total costs per award
deadlineInfo: FY26 (2026): Pre-application/LOI due September 14, 2026, 5:00 pm ET; full application due September 28, 2026, 11:59 pm ET (via eBRAP/Grants.gov).
eligibility: Investigators within 7 years of their most recent postdoctoral research position, clinical fellowship, or equivalent (cannot still be in a postdoc/clinical fellowship at the application deadline). Requires a qualified Designated Mentor and preliminary data. Supports a research project plus structured career development toward independence.
description: DoD KCRP mechanism pairing a research grant with the Academy of Kidney Cancer Investigators career-development program. Foreign and domestic organizations eligible; no U.S. citizenship requirement. One of three FY26 KCRP mechanisms (also Concept Award and Idea Development Award), representing ~$10.2M in anticipated FY26 funding. FY26 announcements released June 18, 2026.

name: AGA-Caroline Craig Augustyn & Damian Augustyn Award in Digestive Cancer
deadlineInfo: 2027 funding cycle: application window opens April 28, 2026 and closes September 15, 2026; notifications by January 31, 2027; funding begins April 1, 2027. Annual cycle.
eligibility: Early-career investigators (no more than 7 years post-training/post-PhD at the April 2027 start) holding an MD, PhD, or equivalent, in a full-time faculty (or equivalent) position at a North American institution (US, Canada, Mexico). Must currently hold an NIH K-series (K01/K08/K23/K99/R00) or other federal/non-federal career-development award of >=4 years duration, with >=1 year remaining as of April 1, 2026. AGA membership required at time of submission.
description: Supplemental award providing additional funding to early-career investigators who already hold a career-development award and conduct research on the pathogenesis, prevention, diagnosis, or treatment of digestive cancer. Applies via the AGA Grants Management System. Citizenship note: requires a full-time faculty position at a North American institution but no explicit citizenship mandate.

name: Team Science Awards
awardAmount: Up to $300,000/year for 3 years (up to ~$900,000 total)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2025-2026 cycle deadlines have passed (LOI Sept 25, 2025; invited full proposals Jan 8, 2026). The 2026-2027 RFP was not yet posted as of July 2026; a new RFP is typically released in summer with an LOI in ~late September - see site.
eligibility: Collaborative, multi-investigator teams (typically combining established and early-career investigators) proposing high-impact melanoma research. Two-stage process (LOI then invited full proposal). Includes an Academic-Industry Partnership Award variant.
description: MRA's team-based mechanism under the general RFP funding collaborative melanoma research teams. MRA accepts proposals from researchers worldwide (no citizenship requirement). Award amounts/durations verified from recent MRA RFP materials; confirm exact 2026-2027 figures and dates when the new RFP posts.

name: General RFP - Young Investigator Award
awardAmount: Up to $85,000/year for 3 years (up to ~$255,000 total)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2025-2026 cycle deadlines have passed (eligibility checklist & biosketch Sept 30, 2025; full proposals Nov 6, 2025). The 2026-2027 RFP was not yet posted as of July 2026; new RFP typically released in summer with proposals due in the fall - see site.
eligibility: Young Investigators - scientists within 5 years of their first academic faculty appointment - proposing innovative melanoma research (MD, PhD, or equivalent).
description: Early-career faculty mechanism under MRA's general RFP supporting independent melanoma research by investigators within 5 years of their first faculty appointment. MRA accepts proposals from researchers worldwide (no citizenship requirement). Award amounts/durations verified from recent MRA RFP materials; confirm exact 2026-2027 figures and dates when the new RFP posts.

name: Career Development Award
funder: American Society for Clinical Oncology (ASCO) / Conquer Cancer
deadlineInfo: October 8, 2026, 11:59 PM EDT (application portal opens July 1, 2026; annual cycle)
eligibility: Physician (MD, DO, or international equivalent) in the 1st-4th year of a full-time primary faculty appointment in a clinical department at an academic medical institution at time of submission; valid, active medical license in the country where research is conducted; completed productive postdoctoral research. Early-career clinical faculty.
description: Funds clinical investigators who have received their initial faculty appointment as they establish an independent clinical cancer research program. Administered through Conquer Cancer, the ASCO Foundation. U.S. and international applicants encouraged; all materials in English. Verified via the CDA application portal: cycle opens July 1, 2026 with an October 8, 2026 deadline.

name: Lustgarten Foundation-AACR Career Development Award for Pancreatic Cancer Research, in Honor of Ruth Bader Ginsburg
deadlineInfo: Annual; 2026-cycle application deadline was January 6, 2026, 1:00 PM ET (now passed). Next cycle expected ~January 2027 (unconfirmed).
eligibility: Assistant professor rank at grant start (research assistant professor, adjunct assistant professor, assistant professor research track, and visiting professor appointments eligible; progression to associate professor disqualifies). Doctoral degree (PhD, MD, MD/PhD, or equivalent); AACR Active member; member of the Women in Cancer Research (WICR) constituency group; independent lab space. Early-career faculty.
description: Supports early-career faculty pursuing pancreatic cancer research (basic, translational, clinical, or population science) aligned with Lustgarten Foundation priorities. No citizenship or geographic restrictions; international applicants must ensure visa status permits completion of the grant term. Award page still displayed the Jan 6, 2026 deadline (grant term starting July 1, 2026) at time of check; the next-cycle date was not yet posted, so no concrete 2027 date is asserted.

name: Clinician Scientist Development Grant (CSDG)
awardAmount: $135,000/year in direct costs plus 8% allowable indirect costs
deadlineInfo: December 1, 2026 (recurring twice-yearly deadlines: June 1 and December 1)
eligibility: Full-time faculty clinician-scientists (doctoral degree, active clinical license, engaged in clinical care) seeking to become independent investigators. Cannot be current/former PI of a mentored training grant or have led an independent extramural grant exceeding 1 year and $100k direct costs. Must be at a U.S. academic institution or eligible non-profit. Early-career faculty.
description: ACS grant helping full-time clinical faculty transition to independent cancer research while maintaining clinical practice. No individual U.S.-citizenship requirement, but the applicant must hold a position at a U.S. institution. Standing biannual deadlines (June 1 / December 1); next upcoming as of mid-2026 is December 1, 2026.

name: Research Scholar Grants (RSG)
awardAmount: Up to $215,000/year in direct costs plus 10% allowable indirect costs
deadlineInfo: December 1, 2026 (recurring twice-yearly deadlines: June 1 and December 1)
eligibility: Independent, full-time faculty (or equivalent) first appointed 8 or fewer years ago; PI on no more than one R01 or R01-equivalent grant at time of application. Institution must provide space/resources customary for independent researchers. Must be at a U.S. academic institution or eligible non-profit. Early-career independent investigators.
description: ACS's flagship investigator-initiated grant for early-career independent faculty (within 8 years of first faculty appointment) across any area of cancer research. No individual U.S.-citizenship requirement, but the applicant must be at a U.S. institution. Standing biannual deadlines (June 1 / December 1); next upcoming as of mid-2026 is December 1, 2026.

name: PCF Challenge Awards
awardAmount: Up to ~$1,000,000 total costs per team over 2 years (no indirect costs); PCF awarded $12M total across the 2025 Challenge Awards
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2026 cycle: portal opened Jan 12, 2026; application deadline was March 16, 2026, 3:00 p.m. EDT (now closed). Next (2027) cycle expected to open ~January 2027 with a March 2027 deadline.
eligibility: Research teams of at least 3 investigators at non-profit academic/medical research centers worldwide, required to include at least one young investigator; focused on metastatic/lethal prostate cancer
description: Team-science award of the Prostate Cancer Foundation for large-scale metastatic prostate cancer research. Note: the current 2026 Challenge Awards RFA is administered by PCF; a Pfizer co-branding is not indicated in the 2026 cycle (historically PCF ran a co-sponsored PCF-Pfizer award). This is a team/faculty award (must include a young investigator) rather than a standalone postdoc award. Applications via pcfscience.org. The 2026 deadline (March 16, 2026) has passed; treat as recurring annual.

name: Damon Runyon Fellowship Award
awardAmount: Stipend $70,000 (yr1), $72,000 (yr2), $74,000 (yr3), $76,000 (yr4), plus $2,000/year expense allowance; up to $100,000 in medical school loan repayment for eligible physician-scientists
deadlineInfo: Full application due August 14, 2026, 4:00 p.m. Eastern Time (submitted via ProposalCentral; no separate LOI). Award runs on a twice-yearly cycle (typically March and August deadlines).
eligibility: Postdoctoral fellows with MD, PhD, MD/PhD, DDS, DVM, or DO. Basic scientists: degree within ~18 months of deadline and less than one year in the sponsor's lab at time of application. Physician-scientists must have completed clinical training and be board-eligible; >=80% research time.
description: Prestigious postdoctoral fellowship for early-career basic and physician-scientists doing cancer research. No citizenship requirement: foreign nationals may apply but must conduct their research at a U.S. institution. Next deadline August 14, 2026 is upcoming as of July 2026.

name: Mentored Investigator Award
awardAmount: $100,000 total (flexible over one or two years)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle (now named 'Mentored Investigator Grant'). 2026 cycle deadlines have passed (LOI ~March 25, 2026; full proposal ~June 10, 2026, per OCRA apply page). Next cycle expected ~early 2027. Applications via SmartSimple.
eligibility: Trainees (postdoctoral fellows or clinical fellows) with an MD or PhD, working under a mentor who is a recognized leader in ovarian cancer research at a non-profit institution
description: RENAMED: formerly the 'Ann and Sol Schreiber Mentored Investigator Award,' now titled 'Mentored Investigator Grant.' Mentored postdoc/clinical-fellow award for ovarian and related gynecologic cancer research. International applicants welcome (institution must be a non-profit research center). 2026 cycle is closed; recurring annually. Some third-party listings cite a June 23, 2026 LOI, but OCRA's own apply page lists the ~March 25, 2026 LOI for this grant, hence medium confidence on the exact cycle timing.

name: Research Grant Awards
awardAmount: Three grants: two of $50,000 (McDermott Family Research Grant Award; Todd Nagel Memorial Research Grant Award) and one of $25,000 (Dr. Marcia Robbins-Wilf Research Grant Award)
deadlineInfo: 2026 cycle open (opened May 2026); application deadline July 19, 2026, 11:59 PM EDT. Winners announced fall 2026.
eligibility: Dermatology residents, fellows, or investigators within 10 years of their first academic appointment; MD, DO, or PhD required; project conducted in or in conjunction with a U.S. dermatology department at a medical/academic institution
description: Annual one-year seed grants for early-career skin cancer research. No stated citizenship restriction, but the research must be conducted at a U.S. dermatology department. The 2026 application deadline (July 19, 2026) is upcoming as of July 2026. Note: the old skincancer.org/for-medical-professionals/research-grants URL now 404s; current page is /about-us/research-grants/ with applications at research.skincancer.org (smapply portal).

name: Career Transition Award
funder: Susan G. Komen Breast Cancer Foundation
awardAmount: Up to $650,000 over 5 years. Phase 1: up to $100,000/year (direct costs) for up to 2 years of mentored postdoc work; Phase 2: up to $150,000/year (direct + indirect) for up to 3 years of independent research
deadlineInfo: FY26 cycle (limited submission - one nominee per institution). Anticipated: institution opt-in ~June 2026; LOI due ~late July 2026 (anticipated ~July 24, 2026); full application (by invitation) ~October 2026; awards notified ~April 2027.
eligibility: Senior postdoctoral/clinical research fellows with no more than 5 years of total postdoctoral research experience at LOI (at least 2 years completed by award start), transitioning to an independent tenure-track position. Limited submission: institution nominates one applicant.
description: Komen award helping senior postdocs launch independent breast cancer research careers, spanning final mentored training (Phase 1) into an independent faculty position (Phase 2). No U.S. citizenship or permanent-residency requirement (unlike the NIH K22). This is a LIMITED SUBMISSION handled through the applicant's institution, so internal deadlines precede the Komen LOI. The July 24, 2026 LOI date is widely reported as 'anticipated' (Komen's FY26 CTA LOI announcement PDF was not directly retrievable), hence medium confidence.

name: Individual Rising Star Grant
funder: The Honorable Tina Brozman Foundation for Ovarian Cancer Research ("Tina's Wish")
awardAmount: $150,000 total over 2 years (per 2024-25 cycle)
deadlineInfo: 2026-27 cycle closed (applications now closed per funder site); grant runs in periodic (~biennial) cycles — next LOI/RFP announced via site and mailing list
eligibility: Early-career researchers at a U.S. school of medicine/public health or U.S. nonprofit research institution; PhD, MD, or MD/PhD; PhD applicants within 3 years of degree and at postdoctoral/non-tenured level when grant begins; committed mentor required
description: Supports early-career investigators bringing fresh approaches to early detection or prevention of ovarian cancer (part of Tina's Wish Rising Star & Team Science grants). Requires a U.S. institution and a committed mentor, but no citizenship requirement. The 2026-27 cycle is closed; interested applicants are directed to grants@tinaswish.org / the mailing list for the next cycle. Exact next deadline not published — do not assume a date.

name: Emerging Leader Award
funder: The Mark Foundation for Cancer Research
deadlineInfo: 2026 cycle: LOI portal opened Mar 25, 2026, LOI due Apr 30, 2026 5 PM ET (now closed); invited full applications due Jul 30, 2026 5 PM ET; award start Jan 1, 2027. Annual program — next LOI cycle ~ spring 2027
eligibility: Independent lab established 3-8 years prior to Dec 31, 2026 (independent appointment in 2018-2023); MD, PhD, or equivalent; single investigator only (no co-investigators)
description: Flagship early-career faculty award. New in 2026: expanded worldwide with NO geography/citizenship restrictions and increased to $1M over 4 years. Full applications are by invitation only after LOI review. For the 2026 cycle the LOI window has closed; the listed Jul 30, 2026 date is the invited full-application deadline. Fresh applicants would enter at the next (~2027) LOI cycle.

name: Heart Disease, Cancer and AIDS Research Program
awardAmount: Typically $100,000-$125,000 (includes 10% indirect costs)
deadlineInfo: Annual application deadline July 15 by 11:59 p.m. EST; next: Jul 15, 2026 (decisions at fall Trustee meeting, notification ~Dec 1)
eligibility: Individual investigators at 501(c)(3) research institutions located in the 5-county Greater Philadelphia region or the City of Camden, NJ; one application per discipline per institution; oriented toward newer investigators seeking seed funding
description: One-year grants for the work of individual investigators, with cancer as one of three eligible focus areas. IMPORTANT geographic restriction: only institutions in the Greater Philadelphia five-county region or Camden, NJ are eligible (this excludes most Johns Hopkins/Baltimore applicants). No citizenship requirement. Next deadline (Jul 15, 2026) is imminent.

name: Lung Cancer Research Program Career Development Award
awardAmount: N/A for FY26 (mechanism not offered this cycle)
deadlineInfo: Not offered in the FY26 LCRP portfolio; last offered ~FY23-FY24. CDMRP mechanisms rotate by fiscal year — watch the LCRP site for future cycles
eligibility: Historically early-career independent investigators working under an experienced mentor; CDMRP awards generally carry no U.S. citizenship requirement
description: The FY26 Lung Cancer Research Program does NOT include a Career Development Award. Confirmed FY26 LCRP mechanisms are: Idea Development Award, Translational Research Award (Level 1), Translational Research Award (Level 2 – Clinical Trial Option), and Patient-Centered Outcomes and Survivorship Award. The CDA may return in a future fiscal year; it is not currently open. No verified next deadline — do not assume one.

name: Lung Cancer Research Program - Idea Development Award
awardAmount: Up to $800,000 total costs
deadlineInfo: FY26: pre-application (required) due Jun 23, 2026 5 PM ET (now closed); full application due Sep 02, 2026 11:59 p.m. ET (only if pre-application/LOI was submitted by the pre-app deadline). Offered annually by fiscal year
eligibility: Independent investigators (per prior LCRP NOFOs, open to investigators at all academic/career levels, not restricted to early career); final FY26 eligibility set in the NOFO. No U.S. citizenship requirement (CDMRP)
description: Active in FY26. Supports innovative, potentially high-impact lung cancer research; not restricted to early-career (the JHU listing tags it early-career, but the mechanism is open to independent investigators at all levels). The FY26 pre-application (required to submit a full application) closed Jun 23, 2026; invited full applications are due Sep 02, 2026.

name: THRIVE Research Grant
funder: HESI (Health and Environmental Sciences Institute; formerly ILSI HESI)
awardAmount: $50,000 USD per awardee (direct costs)
deadlineInfo: 2026 cycle: LOI due May 11, 2026 (closed); invited full proposals due Jul 27, 2026; award decisions Oct 9, 2026. Annual program — next LOI ~ spring 2027
eligibility: M.D., Ph.D., D.V.M., or equivalent degree (degree candidates may apply alongside their PI); faculty or research-institute appointment; able to conduct independent research with peer-reviewed publications
description: Competitive seed grant supporting basic, clinical, and translational research to predict, reduce, or prevent adverse events and long-term/late effects of life-saving cancer therapy (cancer survivorship). Open internationally with no citizenship restriction. Full proposals are by invitation after LOI review; for 2026 the LOI window has closed and the Jul 27, 2026 date is the invited full-proposal deadline.

name: Young Investigator Award
awardAmount: Up to $60,000 per year (up to $180,000 total)
deadlineInfo: Annual; full application due ~mid-December (2026 grant cycle closed Dec 11, 2025 at 8:00 p.m. ET; awards announced May 2026). Next cycle (2027 grant) application expected ~December 2026.
eligibility: MD, MD/PhD, or PhD applicants during fellowship training or early research career; must not hold an appointment higher than Instructor; within 10 years of terminal degree and 5 years of first Assistant Professor appointment at submission. Applicant institution must be in the US or Canada. Cannot hold NIH R/P/F/K awards (institutional T32/K12/KL2 allowed).
description: ALSF's flagship pediatric-oncology grant for scientists moving from training toward research independence, funding basic, translational, or clinical childhood-cancer research. Submitted via ProposalCentral. US/Canadian institutions; US citizenship not required.

name: Endocrine Section Pilot Grants
awardAmount: AHNS Pilot Grant up to $20,000; AHNS Endocrine Surgery Section awards (Eisai, Stryker, ThyCa) ~$10,000 each
deadlineInfo: Annual, administered through the AAO-HNSF CORE program; Letter of Intent ~December 15 and full application ~January 15 (2026 cycle: LOI Dec 15, 2025 / full app Jan 15, 2026, now closed; notifications June). Next cycle LOI expected ~December 2026.
eligibility: Applicant must reside in the US or Canada and be an AHNS member in good standing; medical students, residents, PhDs, or faculty at rank of associate professor or below. Endocrine Surgery Section awards focus on thyroid/endocrine head-and-neck disease; preference to early-stage investigators (within 10 years of terminal degree/fellowship). Cannot already hold private/federal funding for the same project.
description: AHNS research awards administered via the AAO-HNSF Centralized Otolaryngology Research Efforts (CORE) program on ProposalCentral. The general AHNS Pilot Grant supports head-and-neck oncology pilot projects ($20,000); the AHNS Endocrine Surgery Section separately offers Eisai, Stryker, and ThyCa thyroid/endocrine research awards (~$10,000). US/Canada residency required; no citizenship requirement.

name: Familial RUNX1 Research Grant
awardAmount: Early Career Investigator Grant: up to $180,000 over 3 years (max $60,000/yr); Exploration Grant also offered
deadlineInfo: Annual; 2026 program guidelines posted. Exact next deadline see ALSF grants calendar (annual cycle).
eligibility: Early Career Investigator Grant: MD, PhD, MD/PhD (DO, MBBS or equivalent) within 5 years of first Assistant Professor or equivalent tenure-track appointment. Applicant institution must be in the US or Canada; applicants need not be US citizens. Research on inherited RUNX1 mutations / familial platelet disorder (FPD) with predisposition to AML.
description: Now delivered through ALSF's RARE Grant Program (Research Accelerating RUNX1 Exploration), a partnership with the RUNX1 Research Program (formerly Babich Family Foundation). Includes the RUNX1 Early Career Investigator Grant (early-career faculty) and a RUNX1 Exploration Grant. US/Canadian nonprofit institutions; US citizenship not required.

name: AACR-Sontag Foundation Brain Cancer Research Fellowship
deadlineInfo: Annual; competitive Letter of Intent ~mid-January, invited full application ~May 1, grant term starts Aug 1 (2025 cycle: LOI Jan 14, 2025; full app May 1, 2025). The 2026-cycle LOI (~Jan 2026) has closed; next LOI expected ~January 2027.
eligibility: Postdoctoral or clinical research fellow holding a doctoral degree (PhD, MD, MD/PhD or equivalent) and not a candidate for a further doctoral degree; most recent doctoral degree or medical residency completed within the past 5 years (parental/leave exceptions); working under a mentor. Must be an AACR member in good standing. No citizenship or geographic requirement (mentor institution may be anywhere in the world).
description: Two-year mentored fellowship for a postdoc/clinical fellow conducting basic, clinical, translational, or population-science brain-cancer research. Applications from investigators trained in other cancer/biomedical areas applying ideas to brain cancer are encouraged. Truly international eligibility with no citizenship or geographic restriction.

name: ASH Research Training Award for Fellows (RTAF)
awardAmount: $70,000 for one year; plus $1,000 for ASH Annual Meeting travel the following year; second-year support available for up to 4 recipients annually
duration: 1 year (possible second-year support for up to 4 recipients)
deadlineInfo: Annual; application due ~mid-January (2026 cycle due Jan 15, 2026, funding starts July 2026). Next cycle (2027) closes ~January 2027.
eligibility: MD and MD/PhD researchers between 0 and 4 years from completing residency as of the application deadline, in hematology, hematology/oncology, or hematology-related training programs (a temporary exception slightly beyond 4 years applies for the 2027 cycle). Training program based in the US or Canada.
description: Encourages junior physician-scientist fellows to pursue academic hematology by guaranteeing ~12 months of dedicated research time free of heavy clinical duties. US/Canada training programs; no citizenship requirement stated.

name: Postdoctoral Fellowship
awardAmount: $56,000 (year 1), $59,000 (year 2), $62,000 (year 3); plus $1,500/year for conference travel
deadlineInfo: Annual; abstract/Letter of Intent due September 24, full application and reference letters due late January (~Jan 25). Standing annual abstract deadline is September 24, so next abstract deadline is ~September 24, 2026.
eligibility: MD, PhD, MD/PhD, DDS, or DVM (or equivalent; foreign-trained physician equivalency case-by-case). May apply before or during postdoc but, if already appointed, must be within 2 years in the sponsor's lab as of Sept 24. Must work under a sponsor with a formal appointment; research must be performed at a US non-profit university, hospital, or research institution. Ineligible if already accepted another postdoctoral fellowship award.
description: Three-year fellowship for postdoctoral scientists proposing highly innovative work on difficult-to-treat and rare cancers. No citizenship requirement, but research must be conducted at a US non-profit institution. Deadline of Sept 24 is the funder's standing annual abstract deadline.

name: Residents/Fellows in Radiation Oncology Research Seed Grant
deadlineInfo: Annual; complete application due ~late January (2026 cycle due Jan 26, 2026; award notifications Spring 2026). Next cycle deadline expected ~January 2027.
eligibility: Must be enrolled in a US radiation oncology residency or fellowship (or related trainee position) at the time of application; mentored research trainee developing any area that can advance radiation oncology. A single application may be considered for multiple seed-grant categories (e.g., the ASTRO-LUNGevity seed grant for lung-cancer projects, or the ASTRO-AAPM physics seed grant).
description: ASTRO Foundation seed grant supporting residents/fellows in early independent research relevant to radiation oncology. US-based residency/fellowship enrollment required; no explicit citizenship requirement. Submitted through ASTRO's online system.

name: AACR Career Development Awards for Translational Breast Cancer Research
funder: American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) / Breast Cancer Research Foundation (BCRF)
deadlineInfo: Annual cycle. 2026 cycle: LOI Dec 16, 2025 (1 pm ET); full application Apr 7, 2026 (1 pm ET); grant start Jul 1, 2026 (both now past). Next cycle LOI expected ~mid-December 2026.
eligibility: Faculty at assistant professor/instructor/research assistant professor level (or equivalent) who started their first independent faculty position within the past 6 years of the grant start date; active AACR membership required by LOI deadline. Basic, translational, clinical, epidemiological, or population/community breast cancer research. No citizenship or geographic requirement.
description: Now officially titled the 'Breast Cancer Research Foundation-AACR Career Development Awards.' This is the continuation of the historical AACR/BCRF Career Development Awards for Translational Breast Cancer Research (2020 recipients were named under that exact title). Program is active and recurs annually; the 'translational breast cancer research' phrasing was dropped from the name but the award scope and funders are the same.

name: Investigator-Initiated Research Grant
awardAmount: Up to $150,000 plus 10% indirect (2-year); limited number of 3-year grants up to $225,000 plus 10% indirect
deadlineInfo: Annual, two-stage (LOI then full application). LOI opens ~early December and closes ~mid-January (2025 cycle LOI closed Jan 17, 2025); full applications ~summer; decisions ~January. Next new-applicant entry point: LOI ~mid-January 2027.
eligibility: Independent Investigators with PhD/MD or equivalent at a non-profit academic/research institution, at the level of Assistant Professor (or equivalent) or higher. Research on modifiable lifestyle exposures (diet, nutrition, physical activity, body weight) and cancer risk/survivorship. Geographic restriction: applications accepted only from the Americas (North/Central/South America and the Caribbean); no U.S. citizenship requirement.
description: AICR's flagship grant program supporting research on how modifiable lifestyle factors affect cancer risk and outcomes. Active and recurring annually via a two-stage LOI/full-application process. Note the geographic (Americas-only) restriction even though there is no citizenship requirement.

name: Innovative Testing Research
funder: Earlier.org - Friends For An Earlier Breast Cancer Test
deadlineInfo: See site. Seed/pilot grants awarded on an ongoing basis via an application process (guidance in 'Resources for Prospective Researchers'); no fixed public calendar deadline found.
eligibility: Pilot/seed-stage projects developing new methods to improve detection of early breast cancer, with particular interest in biological or immunologic methods of early detection. Open to researchers; specific career-stage and citizenship requirements are not stated publicly.
description: Small foundation (est. 1995, Greensboro NC) that funds seed grants for novel early breast cancer detection methods; has awarded 138 grants totaling over $5.2 million. Program remains active. Award ceiling ~$40,000 over 1-3 years. No concrete application deadline is published on the site; prospective applicants are directed to the foundation's prospective-researcher resources, so the deadline is set to 'see site' with lowered confidence.

name: Hollis Brownstein Research Grants Program
awardAmount: Up to $150,000 (per current guidelines; historically $100,000)
deadlineInfo: Annual. 2026 cycle: LOI opened Dec 5, 2025 and was due Feb 6, 2026 (12 pm CT); invited full applications due Apr 13, 2026 (12 pm CT); awards by Jun 30, 2026; funding starts Jul 1, 2026 (all now past). Next cycle LOI expected to open ~December 2026, due ~early February 2027.
eligibility: New Investigators - independent investigators at a non-profit academic institution within 7 years after the start date of their first independent faculty-level appointment (training years excluded). No renewals; prior LRF New Investigator awardees ineligible. No citizenship requirement - applicants need not be U.S. citizens and projects from outside the U.S. are eligible.
description: The Leukemia Research Foundation's New Investigator Research Grants, offered under the Hollis Brownstein Research Grants Program, support new independent investigators studying blood cancers. Active and recurring annually via a two-stage LOI/full-application process on ProposalCentral. Award terms shown on the current guidelines page (up to $150,000 / 2 years) differ from some older sources citing $100,000 / 1 year.

name: LCRF Minority Career Development Award (CDA) in Lung Cancer
awardAmount: $150,000 ($75,000/year; no indirect costs)
deadlineInfo: Annual, two-stage (LOI then invited full proposal via ProposalCentral). Program active (2025 awardee named; an additional CDA awarded early 2026). It was not among LCRF's July 2026 open RFPs (ALK-positive and SCLC awards, LOI due Jul 15, 2026), so consult the LCRF funding-opportunities page for the current MCDA RFP and dates.
eligibility: Early-stage scientists from racial/ethnic groups underrepresented in biomedical research (Black/African American, Hispanic/Latino, American Indian/Alaskan Native, Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander), or otherwise demonstrably underrepresented at their host institution. Must be postdoctoral researchers, clinical fellows, or early/mid-career investigators within 10 years of receiving MD and/or PhD (exceptions possible). Supported in part by the LRE Foundation.
description: Two-year career development award to build protected time and mentoring for lung-cancer researchers from underrepresented groups. Confirmed active per the 2025 LCRF Minority CDA RFP. Applicants from U.S.-based and international institutions are eligible and may hold any residency/citizenship status (no citizenship requirement); the restriction is demonstrated minority/underrepresented status, not nationality. Applicants may apply for only one LCRF grant per cycle and cannot have received LRF/LCRF funding in the last 4 years.
